factors.summary: Cost reduction programs are delivering measurable savings, with selling, general, and administrative expenses decreasing as a percentage of net sales.

evidence.text: As a percentage of net sales, selling, general and administrative expenses decreased to 15.9% in the third quarter of 2021 from 18.8% in the third quarter of 2020, largely driven by cost leverage on increased net sales and disciplined expense management.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2021 Compared with the Three Months Ended September 30, 2020

factors.summary: Productivity is improving, contributing to cost leverage and margin expansion.

evidence.text: As a percentage of net sales, selling, general and administrative expenses decreased to 15.9% in the third quarter of 2021 from 18.8% in the third quarter of 2020, largely driven by cost leverage on increased net sales and disciplined expense management.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2021 Compared with the Three Months Ended September 30, 2020

factors.summary: Management is confident in the long-term outlook, with expectations for continued growth.

evidence.text: We believe the long-term outlook for the housing industry is positive due to growth in the underlying demographics compared to historical new construction levels, despite the uncertainty in the industry at the outset of the COVID-19 pandemic. We feel we are well-positioned to take advantage of the construction activity in our markets and to increase our market share, which may include strategic acquisitions.

factors.summary: The company is gaining market share, particularly with large production homebuilders.

evidence.text: Our focus is on maintaining relationships and market share with these customers while balancing the competitive pressures we are facing in servicing large homebuilders with certain profitability expectations. Additionally, we have been successful in expanding our custom homebuilder base while maintaining acceptable credit standards.

factors.summary: The competitive environment is intensifying due to industry consolidation and new entrants.

evidence.text: There has been a trend of consolidation within the building products supply industry. However, our industry remains highly fragmented and competitive and we will continue to face significant competition from local and regional suppliers.

factors.summary: Construction activity is a tailwind, with strong demand for building materials.

evidence.text: Our net sales for the third quarter of 2021 increased 140.0% from the same period last year. The significant increase was primarily driven by acquisitions, with our BMC Merger accounting for 77.3% of our sales growth in the third quarter of 2021, while commodity price inflation accounted for another 38.6%. The remainder of the increase was a result of core organic sales growth in our single family customer segment.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is a consideration, with the company optimizing its capital structure.

evidence.text: As a result of our historical growth through acquisitions, we had $2.5 billion of indebtedness as of September 30, 2021. We strive to optimize our capital structure to ensure that our financial needs are met in light of economic conditions, business activities, organic investments, opportunities for growth through acquisition and the overall risk characteristics of our underlying assets.

factors.summary: The company is repurchasing shares, signaling confidence in its future performance.

evidence.text: During the three months ended September 30, 2021, the Company repurchased 10,958,400 shares at a weighted average price of $52.74 per share for a total cost of approximately $578.2 million, net of fees.

factors.summary: The order backlog is growing, driven by strong demand and acquisitions, with new orders outpacing revenue growth.

evidence.text: We achieved increased net sales in all of our product categories, primarily due to the BMC Merger, commodity inflation, and core organic sales growth.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2021 Compared with the Three Months Ended September 30, 2020

evidence.text: Our net sales for the third quarter of 2021 increased 140.0% from the same period last year. The significant increase was primarily driven by acquisitions, with our BMC Merger accounting for 77.3% of our sales growth in the third quarter of 2021, while commodity price inflation accounted for another 38.6%. The remainder of the increase was a result of core organic sales growth in our single family customer segment.

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting positively towards higher-margin segments, particularly in value-added product categories, which has contributed to an increase in gross margin percentage.

evidence.text: Gross margin percentage increased to 31.1% in the third quarter of 2021 from 24.9% in the third quarter of 2020, a 6.2% increase. This increase was primarily attributable to pricing relative to the impact of commodity price inflation, as well as core organic growth particularly in value-added product categories.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2021 Compared with the Three Months Ended September 30, 2020

evidence.text: Our gross margin percentage in the third quarter of 2021 increased by 6.2% compared to the third quarter of 2020 primarily due to effective pricing relative to the impact of commodity inflation, as well as core organic growth in value-added product categories.

factors.summary: The company has demonstrated strong pricing power, successfully raising prices to outpace commodity inflation and achieve higher gross margins. Positive customer response has further supported this strategy.

evidence.text: Gross margin percentage increased to 31.1% in the third quarter of 2021 from 24.9% in the third quarter of 2020, a 6.2% increase. This increase was primarily attributable to pricing relative to the impact of commodity price inflation, as well as core organic growth particularly in value-added product categories.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2021 Compared with the Three Months Ended September 30, 2020

evidence.text: Our gross margin percentage in the third quarter of 2021 increased by 6.2% compared to the third quarter of 2020 primarily due to effective pricing relative to the impact of commodity inflation, as well as core organic growth in value-added product categories.

factors.summary: Demand is accelerating, supported by strong net sales growth across all product categories and increasing housing starts, particularly in the single-family sector.

evidence.text: Net sales for the three months ended September 30, 2021 were $5.5 billion, a 140.0% increase over net sales of $2.3 billion for the three months ended September 30, 2020. The BMC Merger and commodity price inflation increased net sales by 77.3% and 38.6%, respectively. The remaining increase in net sales is primarily attributable to core organic growth in our single family customer segment and net sales from our other acquisitions.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2021 Compared with the Three Months Ended September 30, 2020

evidence.text: According to the U.S. Census Bureau, actual U.S. total housing starts were 0.4 million for the third quarter of 2021, an increase of 8.5% compared to the third quarter of 2020. Actual U.S. single-family starts for the third quarter of 2021 were 0.3 million, an increase of 4.7% compared to the third quarter of 2020.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, driven by both organic growth and external factors such as housing market trends.

evidence.text: We achieved increased net sales in all of our product categories, primarily due to the BMC Merger, commodity inflation, and core organic sales growth.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2021 Compared with the Three Months Ended September 30, 2020

Actual U.S. single-family starts for the third quarter of 2021 were 0.3 million, an increase of 4.7% compared to the third quarter of 2020.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating and contributing to overall revenue increases. The growth is supported by strong housing starts and market expansion, particularly in the single family customer segment.

evidence.text: The remaining increase in net sales is primarily attributable to core organic growth in our single family customer segment and net sales from our other acquisitions.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2021 Compared with the Three Months Ended September 30, 2020

The significant increase was primarily driven by acquisitions, with our BMC Merger accounting for 77.3% of our sales growth in the third quarter of 2021, while commodity price inflation accounted for another 38.6%. The remainder of the increase was a result of core organic sales growth in our single family customer segment.

factors.summary: The single-family customer segment is the fastest-growing end market, contributing significantly to revenue increases. The market experienced strong demand and organic growth, with acquisitions and commodity price inflation also contributing to sales growth.

evidence.text: The remaining increase in net sales is primarily attributable to core organic growth in our single family customer segment and net sales from our other acquisitions.

The remainder of the increase was a result of core organic sales growth in our single family customer segment.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising, primarily driven by increases in lumber prices and supply chain disruptions. Despite these challenges, the company has managed to offset the cost increases through effective pricing strategies, leading to an improvement in gross margins.

evidence.text: Gross margin percentage increased to 31.1% in the third quarter of 2021 from 24.9% in the third quarter of 2020, a 6.2% increase. This increase was primarily attributable to pricing relative to the impact of commodity price inflation, as well as core organic growth particularly in value-added product categories.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2021 Compared with the Three Months Ended September 30, 2020

evidence.text: Our gross margin percentage in the third quarter of 2021 increased by 6.2% compared to the third quarter of 2020 primarily due to effective pricing relative to the impact of commodity inflation, as well as core organic growth in value-added product categories.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving and favorable, supported by strong sales and margin improvements, with cash provided by operating activities increasing significantly year-over-year.

evidence.text: Cash provided by operating activities was $903.2 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2021 compared to cash provided by operating activities of $155.1 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2020. The increase in cash provided by operating activities was largely the result of an increase in net income in the first nine months of 2021, exceeding the net income in the first nine months of 2020.

Our gross margin percentage in the third quarter of 2021 increased by 6.2% compared to the third quarter of 2020 primarily due to effective pricing relative to the impact of commodity inflation, as well as core organic growth in value-added product categories.

factors.summary: The company is actively engaging in mergers and acquisitions, which have significantly contributed to its growth and market expansion. Recent developments include the BMC Merger and four other acquisitions, all accounted for using the acquisition method of accounting. These activities have led to substantial increases in net sales, driven by both organic growth and the integration of new acquisitions.

evidence.text: The BMC Merger and commodity price inflation increased net sales by 77.3% and 38.6%, respectively. The remaining increase in net sales is primarily attributable to core organic growth in our single family customer segment and net sales from our other acquisitions.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2021 Compared with the Three Months Ended September 30, 2020

evidence.text: During the nine months ended September 30, 2021, we have also completed four other acquisitions. The BMC Merger and the other acquisitions were accounted for using the acquisition method of accounting, with Builders FirstSource, Inc. as the accounting acquirer.

factors.summary: The margin trajectory is positive, with both gross and operating margins expanding. This improvement is attributed to pricing power, cost leverage, disciplined expense management, effective pricing strategies, and organic growth despite challenges like commodity inflation.

evidence.text: Gross margin percentage increased to 31.1% in the third quarter of 2021 from 24.9% in the third quarter of 2020, a 6.2% increase.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2021 Compared with the Three Months Ended September 30, 2020

evidence.text: As a percentage of net sales, selling, general and administrative expenses decreased to 15.9% in the third quarter of 2021 from 18.8% in the third quarter of 2020, largely driven by cost leverage on increased net sales and disciplined expense management.

Our gross margin percentage in the third quarter of 2021 increased by 6.2% compared to the third quarter of 2020 primarily due to effective pricing relative to the impact of commodity inflation, as well as core organic growth in value-added product categories.
